title: Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers 24 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness HOME FORWARD SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) program helps low-income residents afford rental homes and apartments at normal market rates. Project-based Section 8 assistance is available at many locations throughout Multnomah County. These are apartments where the subsidy stays with the unit (it can not be taken by the resident to another location). Listings of apartment vacancies are updated approximately every 4-6 weeks on the website. Also accepts calls to report misuse of the program. 135 SW Ash Street Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: -Low-income households -Age 18 or older at the time of application or an emancipated minor under State Law -Individuals who have a terminal illness and have been diagnosed by a medical provider with a life expectancy of 12 months or less may apply for Section 8 under priority status Hours: -Section 8 Housing Voucher program services: Tuesday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm, in person -Section 8 office phone: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm, appointments can be scheduled Email (503) 802-8333 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMES FOR GOOD SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rental assistance to low-income families in the private rental market. A family member must complete a housing application and be placed on a waitlist. When the family reaches the top of the waitlist, income and assets are verified and the family may be considered eligible. The eligible family then locates a unit and a willing owner and enters into a lease agreement; the owner contracts with Homes for Good. Homes for Good pays the owner a portion of the family's rent based on their income. New families are added to the program as vacancies occur. Also fields complaints about Section 8 housing. 100 W 13th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Low-income families, pregnant women, older adults, and adults with disabilities. Eligibility is based on income, criminal history, and citizenship status at the time a household reaches the top of the waitlist. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 682-3755 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, referrals, and support for landlord-tenant issues, fair housing, and complaints of housing discrimination. Maintains listings of low-income housing in Clackamas County, including non-subsidized low-cost apartments. Manages Housing Authority-owned units, subsidized rental housing, and the Section 8/Housing Choice Voucher program. Handles complaints related to public housing and Section 8. 13930 S Gain Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Varies by program or property Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, holiday hours posted on website Email (503) 655-8267 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF DOUGLAS COUNTY SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vouchers to assist low-income households with rental costs. Once an eligible household is issued a voucher for rental assistance, the agency pays a portion of the household's rent directly to the landlords. Also issues both Family Choice Vouchers and HUD VASH Vouchers for veteran families. Additionally, fields general complaints about the Section 8 program. 1000 W Stanton Street Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Must meet income and asset guidelines Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 673-6548 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF JACKSON COUNTY HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance about information and acquisition of Section 8 vouchers and fields complaints about the program. Projected wait times for the program are 4-5 years. 2251 Table Rock Road Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Must meet income guidelines, pass a criminal background check, and have at least one family member who is a citizen or permanent resident of the US. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm. First Thursday of the month 10am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Email (541) 779-5785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF LINCOLN COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about affordable housing. Administers Section 8/Housing Choice Vouchers and public housing, in addition to fielding complaints about those programs. 1039 NW Nye Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Varies by program. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 265-5326 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF MALHEUR AND HARNEY COUNTIES HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the following housing programs: -Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program: As of April 2018, Section 8 is accepting pre-applications for the wait list and has a 12-month wait time -Family Self-Sufficiency Program: Helps Section 8 recipients obtain education and job skills in order to become financially self-sufficient -Housing Choice Voucher Homeownership Program: Helps Section 8 recipients receive assistance toward a monthly mortgage payment -Low-Rent Public Housing Program: Provides federally subsidized rental housing -Farm Labor Housing: Offers housing for active and retired agricultural workers Also fields complaints about Section 8 and public housing programs. 959 Fortner Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: -Low-income households; applicants from outside Malheur and Harney Counties must reside in Malheur or Harney County for one year before requesting to move to another county. -Farm labor housing is available to domestic agricultural workers including those working on fish and oyster farms and on-farm processing; can be retired and/or disabled from agricultural work; must be a U.S. citizen or permanent resident. Available to laborers that handle any raw materials. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 889-9661 101 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF YAMHILL COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) rental assistance, including a Homeownership Voucher Program. Also offers a Family Self-Sufficiency program, Housing Rehab program, and manages several area affordable housing complexes for families, farmworkers, older adults, and people with special needs. 135 NE Dunn Place Mcminnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Varies; some programs may have residency or income requirements. Call for information. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 883-4300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING WORKS BEND SATELITE OFFICE HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER WAITLIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the Housing Choice Voucher/Section 8 program for HUD. The program helps low-income residents afford rental homes and apartments at normal market rates. 1475 NE Forbes Drive Suite 110 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Income must not exceed 50% of the annual median income based on household size Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm. Waitlist opens for a one week period in early January. Email (458) 281-0813 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING WORKS HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER WAITLIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the Housing Choice Voucher/Section 8 program for HUD. The program helps low-income residents afford rental homes and apartments at normal market rates. 405 SW 6th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Income must not exceed 50% of the annual median income based on household size Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm. Waitlist opens for a one week period in early January. Email (541) 923-1018 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOSEPHINE HOUSING AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT COUNCIL HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides rental assistance to low-income families through HUD's Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) program. This federally funded program provides rent subsidies that enable income-qualified participants to lease privately owned rental housing. Fields complaints about Section 8 HCV program. Also owns the 48-unit Harbeck Village apartments and the 56- unit Cathedral Square, affordable housing projects. Does not offer any emergency housing assistance. 1205 NE 7th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Must meet income guidelines. A monthly gross income chart can be viewed on agency website under "Applicants" Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 479-5529 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH HOUSING AUTHORITY SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER AND PROJECT-BASED PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Housing Choice Voucher program (Section 8 Tenant-Based Rental Assistance) provides direct rental subsidy payments on behalf of eligible families and individuals to private landlords. The Section 8 Project-Based Rental Assistance program provides rental assistance on behalf of eligible families or individuals. Project-based assistance is tied to specific multifamily developments. All subsidies remain with the approved development, therefore, once a project-based subsidy tenant moves from the development, the subsidy stays with the development and the tenant no longer receives assistance. Fields complaints about Section 8. 1445 Avalon Street Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: -Must be a US citizen or have an eligible immigration status -Household income must not exceed 50% of the Area Median Income (AMI) Hours: Monday/Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 884-0649 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN BENTON HOUSING AUTHORITY SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers two types of Section 8 housing assistance programs: Housing Choice Vouchers and Moderate Rehabilitation. Housing Choice Vouchers: Eligible families find their own rental units in the existing housing market. Moderate Rehabilitation: LBHA provides a rent subsidy guarantee to units that have been approved and rehabilitated in accordance with Housing and Urban Development (HUD) regulations. 1250 SE Queen Avenue Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 926-4497 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Marion County Housing Authority (MCHA), under contract with the US Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), administers the Section 8 Voucher Program and several other rental assistance programs. MCHA also owns and manages several affordable apartment complexes for seniors, families, and farm laborers. Serves the rural areas of Marion County, which are outside the Urban Growth Boundary of the City of Salem. No assistance is available for Salem/Keizer residents. 2645 Portland Road NE Suite 200 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Eligibility varies by program. HUD-established income limits apply. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm, closed federal holidays Email (503) 798-4170 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA AND COLUMBIA GORGE HOUSING AUTHORITIES HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER SECTION 8 Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides affordable, tenant-based housing assistance for individuals who face barriers due to income, disability, or age. Assistance follows the tenant as long as they remain eligible. 500 E 2nd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Low-income individuals and families Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4pm and by appointment Email (541) 296-5462 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTH BEND CITY COOS-CURRY HOUSING AUTHORITIES HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residents of the community with information about and access to affordable housing. Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program is tenant based assistance, meaning assistance follows the tenant as long as they remain eligible for the program. The Housing Authority is not the landlord but assists with rent payments to the landlords. Public Housing units are owned and managed by the Housing Authority. This program provides unit-based assistance, meaning if a family moves out of the unit, the assistance remains with the unit. One small pet is allowed with prior approval before moving into a unit. 1700 Monroe Street North Bend, OR 97459 Eligibility: Must meet income guidelines Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 12:45pm-4:30pm Email (541) 756-4111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHEAST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers low-income and subsidized apartments. Available programs: Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers, Veterans Supportive Housing Vouchers, Section 8 Housing Voucher Homeownership, Mental Health Referred Housing, Family Self-Sufficiency, HUD Multi-Family Housing, Tax Credit Housing, and Rural Development Housing. 2608 May Lane La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Email (541) 963-5360 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST OREGON HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers federal housing programs. Maintains a list of affordable housing and a bulletin board of local properties, including subsidized properties that are independently managed. Fields complaints about public housing and Section 8 housing. 147 S Main Avenue Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm; by appointment only Email (503) 861-0119 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALEM HOUSING AUTHORITY SECTION 8 HOUSING PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about Section 8 housing in addition to fielding complaints about the program. 360 Church Street SE Salem, OR 973013707 Eligibility: Must meet income guidelines, pass a criminal background check, and have at least one family member who is a citizen or lawful permanent resident of the US Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm. Closed all major holidays. Email (503) 588-6368 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA COUNTY HOUSING AUTHORITY HOUSING AUTHORITY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administers the federal and public housing programs including the Section 8 Housing Voucher program and manages several low-income apartment complexes. Listings of low-income properties, rural areas, and properties for agricultural workers. 155 SW Tenth Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: -Low-income households -Section 8: Income limits are 30% of median household income for the county (chart on website under public housing) -Programs prioritize families with children, older adults, and people with disabilities Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-12pm and 1pm-4pm, Wednesday 1pm-4pm Email (541) 567-3241 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT PORTLAND FIELD OFFICE DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides referrals and information about income-based housing through local Public Housing Authorities and other providers, Federal Housing Administration (FHA) loans, manufactured housing; and referrals to HUD-approved housing counseling agencies for foreclosure, purchase, reverse mortgage, and budgeting assistance. A list of HUD-funded housing is available online. 1220 SW 3rd Avenue Suite 400 Portland, OR 972042825 Eligibility: Income restrictions vary by program. Acceptance of rental application is at apartment manager's discretion. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 222-2600 0 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY DEPARTMENT OF HOUSING SERVICES LOW-INCOME HOUSING ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers housing options such as Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers, Project-Based Vouchers, and Public Housing. 161 NW Adams Avenue Suite 2000 Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Varies by program. Voucher programs serve households with income at or below the HUD Very Low Income limit (50% of area Median Family Income). Public Housing serves households with income at or below the HUD Low Income limit (80% of area Median Family Income). Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 846-4794 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WEST VALLEY HOUSING AUTHORITY SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about Section 8 housing and takes complaints about the program. 204 SW Walnut Avenue Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Income must be at or below 50% of Area Median Income Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (503) 623-8387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOME FORWARD SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM HOMES FOR GOOD SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHER PROGRAM
